title: Smoky Smoked Beef Stir-fry
description: This smoky, juicy Korean dish is perfect for a solo dinner featuring beef that has marinated in a blend of soy sauce and chili paste for extra depth of flavor.

ingredients.name: Vegetables (optional, such as bell peppers or onions)

instructions: Chop the beef into thin slices and marinate with soy sauce, red chilli paste, minced ginger, Korean chili flakes, gochugaru (1 tsp), and water for at least 30 minutes.
Heat a pan over medium-high heat and add vegetable oil. Once hot, sauté the garlic until fragrant.
Add the marinated beef slices to the pan and stir-fry for about 5-7 minutes or until fully cooked but still juicy.
If using vegetables, chop them into bite-sized pieces and add to the pan with the beef. Stir fry together for another minute or two until all ingredients are heated through.
Turn off heat, taste, and adjust seasoning if necessary. Serve hot.
